The theory of relativity, developed by Albert Einstein, is a fundamental concept in modern physics that explains how space and time are connected. I'll break it down into simple terms, covering both special and general relativity with examples.

**Special Relativity (1905)**

Special relativity deals with the behavior of objects when they move at high speeds, close to the speed of light. The key concept is that the laws of physics are the same for all observers, regardless of their relative motion.

**Time Dilation:**

Imagine you have a twin brother, and you both have identical clocks. You stay on Earth, while your twin brother goes on a high-speed spaceship and travels to a distant star at 90% of the speed of light. When he returns, you compare your clocks.

To you on Earth, time has passed normally, and your clock shows a certain time. However, for your twin brother, time has passed slower due to his high-speed motion. When he returns,
